claim_boundary: Ticket 61 replaces TICKET60's replay-derived separator with pre-replay separator tests and transfers that stricter rule to RH, Goldbach, and Twin Prime. It does not prove or disprove any of the four open problems.

attempts.attempt: Transfer TICKET61's stricter rule: a separator that is discovered after replay is not a proof coordinate. Replace it with a pre-observable symbolic coordinate, or promote the failure to a counterexample search target.

bounded_result.symbolic_gate: spectral separator keys must be defined before zero-kernel replay or height-window evaluation
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every mixed zero-kernel lift cylinder has a pre-replay spectral coordinate with a closed positivity transition law, or yields an off-critical packet with stable sign obstruction.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a zero-kernel cylinder whose positivity ambiguity survives all pre-replay symbolic separator keys
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A finite separator is useful only when its key is available before replay and has a symbolic transition law. Otherwise it remains a diagnostic coordinate, not a proof bridge.

attempts.obstruction: This is a proof-discipline transfer and does not solve the target problem. It upgrades the next experiment from diagnostic separation to symbolic transition closure.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every mixed zero-kernel lift cylinder has a pre-replay spectral coordinate with a closed positivity transition law, or yields an off-critical packet with stable sign obstruction.
attempts.next_experiment: Build the problem-specific pre-replay separator table and test symbolic transition closure.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: pre-replay separator audit over selected mixed cylinders
attempts.attempt: Continue from TICKET60 by forbidding replay-derived separator keys and asking whether a pre-replay higher-bit coordinate predicts the failure offset that previously separated the mixed cylinders.

separator_scope.pre_replay_key_rule: Keys may use low40 cylinder identity, certificate prefix length, and high-extension bits. They may not use replay-derived failure_offset, failure_observed, or first_failure certificate data.

symbolic_failure_offset_audit.discarded_route: Use low40+failure_offset from TICKET60 as if it were a proof coordinate. That is circular because failure_offset is learned only after replaying the trajectory to failure.
symbolic_failure_offset_audit.retained_route: Replace the replay-derived coordinate with a pre-replay symbolic coordinate. In the selected data, low40 plus high_extension modulo 16 already determines failure_offset, outcome, and boundary-match status for every mixed start-template lift.
symbolic_failure_offset_audit.candidate_theorem: For every selected mixed low40 cylinder, the mod-16 high-extension residue determines the first failure offset and the boundary prediction label; extend this to a symbolic transition theorem or an automaton-counted cover that excludes full-period nondecreasing cycles.
symbolic_failure_offset_audit.counterexample_target: A selected or newly lifted mixed cylinder whose failure_offset remains ambiguous under low40 plus high_extension mod 16, or a full-period replay inside a mod-16-separated cylinder.
symbolic_failure_offset_audit.proof_boundary: This is finite selected-cylinder evidence. It removes one replay-derived separator from the immediate candidate route, but it does not prove Collatz and does not certify a Collatz counterexample.

attempts.obstruction: The mod-16 separator is still an empirical coordinate on a selected finite cover. It becomes proof material only if a symbolic transition theorem proves that the coordinate is complete under lifts and decreases or enters a counted terminal cover.
attempts.candidate_theorem: For every selected mixed low40 cylinder, the mod-16 high-extension residue determines the first failure offset and the boundary prediction label; extend this to a symbolic transition theorem or an automaton-counted cover that excludes full-period nondecreasing cycles.
attempts.next_experiment: Generate CO-TICKET-62 by deriving the mod-16 high-extension transition table symbolically, then testing whether it closes under larger cylinder lifts or exposes an ambiguous lift.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no certified Collatz counterexample.

bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every mixed Goldbach margin cylinder has a pre-replay character/error coordinate with a closed cutoff transition law, or yields a cutoff-stable exceptional even packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a residue/cutoff cylinder whose positive-margin ambiguity survives all pre-replay character keys
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A finite separator is useful only when its key is available before replay and has a symbolic transition law. Otherwise it remains a diagnostic coordinate, not a proof bridge.

bounded_result.counterexample_target: a sieve cylinder whose gap-2 mass ambiguity survives all pre-replay parity/error keys
